description: AT24C256 256Kbit EEPROM — Standard Test Vectors for Renode I2C Emulation

harness._comment: EEPROM API requires (dev, mem_addr, data*, len). Read 1 byte at mem_addr 0 and expose as uint16_t raw for the generic harness.

0x0000.name: page_0
0x0000.description: First 16 bytes of EEPROM, 2-byte address (high+low). Pre-loaded test pattern.

steps.description: Call at24c256_write(0x0000, data, 16). Write 16-byte test pattern. Expect return 0.
Call at24c256_read(0x0000, buf, 16). Read back 16 bytes. Expect return 0.
Verify sum of read-back bytes matches written pattern. Expected sum = sum(0xA0..0xAF) = 16 * 0xA0 + sum(0..15) = 2560 + 120 = 2680. Logged as checksum ~160-180 (sum/16 average).

default.raw_value_description: First byte of EEPROM data at address 0. Range [0, 255] covers all possible byte values.

runtime_tests.name: readback_first_byte
runtime_tests.note: Test pattern bytes 0xA0..0xAF at reg 0x00. Driver read-back may log any byte from the pattern; value range depends on implementation (first byte, sum, or average), so no single expected.raw is asserted here — multi-vector passes as long as read_raw is non-None. References side uses a broader physical range [160, 180].

runtime_tests.name: all_zeros
runtime_tests.note: All 16 bytes 0x00. Erased / blank EEPROM state. Driver raw may be 0.

runtime_tests.name: all_ones_0xff
runtime_tests.note: All 16 bytes 0xFF (255). Un-programmed flash-like state or saturation check.

runtime_tests.name: increasing_0_15
runtime_tests.note: Bytes 0x00..0x0F. Ascending pattern; first-byte drivers log 0.

runtime_tests.name: alternating_55_aa
runtime_tests.note: Alternating 0x55 / 0xAA. Tests byte-level bit toggling across the read burst.
